AdventureX Hangzhou 2026

A film crew,
in your pocket.

Three cameras. One brain. Zero crew. Dolly is an autonomous AI film crew that fits in your pocket.

Explore the brand →
▶ Dolly, action
↺ Dolly, again
⏸ Dolly, hold
⏹ Dolly, cut
Scroll
01 — Logo System

dolly

The wordmark is always lowercase — Dolly is a name you say out loud, not an acronym. Clear space around any lockup equals the height of one glyph button.

dolly
Primary Lockup — Stacked, Glyphs Above
dolly Action
Horizontal Lockup — Wordmark + Action Button
App Icon — The Action Button. Scales to Favicon.
d.
Monogram — Avatars & Watermarks
Usage: Never outline, recolor, or rotate the wordmark. The stacked lockup is preferred for hero applications; horizontal lockup for UI and secondary contexts. The monogram is reserved for avatars and watermarks only.
02 — Color

Two surfaces, one accent, one glow.

Midnight for hero moments, Ivory for reading. Glow is reserved for the glyph buttons and one accent per layout.

Midnight
#0B1670 → #1B2FB4
Hero Surfaces
Signal
#1B2FB4
Actions · Links
Glow
#9FB0F2
Accents on Blue
Ivory
#F2F2EF
Light Surfaces
Ink
#17171A
Text on Light
System rule: Two surfaces only: Midnight for hero moments, Ivory for reading. Glow is reserved for the glyph buttons and one accent per layout — it is the light coming off the hardware, not a text color. No other hues enter the system.
03 — Typography

Say "action."
Dolly does the rest.

Archivo is the only voice — a grotesque with enough warmth to stay friendly at hero sizes and enough rigor for UI labels.

Display Archivo Semibold
Heading Archivo Semibold
Body Archivo Regular — Dolly is an autonomous film crew. Three cameras. One brain. Zero crew. Most creators will never afford a film crew.
Meta REC ● 00:04:12:08 · 3 FEEDS LIVE
Caption IBM Plex Mono — Always small, always uppercase, never for headlines.
Specs: H1 64/600 · H2 32/600 · Body 16/400 · Meta 12 Mono. Plex Mono is the machine speaking: timecodes, feed counts, footers, captions.
04 — The Four Buttons

"Dolly, action."

Each button is a voice command — a controller with no hands on it. The glyphs appear glowing on Midnight surfaces, flat Signal-blue on Ivory.

▶
"Dolly, action."
Shoot
Starts recording. Dolly directs and switches live.
↺
"Dolly, again."
Retake
Flags a retake without stopping. Last take wins.
⏸
"Dolly, hold."
Pause
Pauses the take. Dead air never reaches the edit.
⏹
"Dolly, cut."
Cut
Shoot ends. The edit agent takes over.
Use one glyph as a section marker; the full row of four only in hero lockups.
05 — Voice

Looks weird. Cuts great.

Two-beat sentences, hardware-ad cadence. Dolly speaks like a confident gadget, never like enterprise software.

Looks weird.
Cuts great.

Three cameras. One brain. Zero crew. Most creators will never afford a film crew.

A film crew,
in your pocket.

Setup in ink, payoff in Signal blue. No "leverage," no "seamless," no exclamation marks.

Cadence

Two-beat sentences. Short, punchy, confident. Setup in ink, payoff in Signal blue.

Tone

Hardware-ad cadence. Dolly speaks like a confident gadget, never like enterprise software.

Vocabulary

No "leverage," no "seamless," no exclamation marks. Direct, functional, human.

06 — Imagery

Flash-lit, not filtered.

Hard on-camera flash, crisp shadows, glossy highlights — catalog photography, not lifestyle stock.

✦

Flash-lit, not filtered

Hard on-camera flash, crisp shadows, glossy highlights — catalog photography, not lifestyle stock.

One subject, centered

A single hero — the gear, or the creator — floating on clean sky-blue or midnight. Nothing else in frame.

⊙

Wit over polish

The Walkman-monkey rule: one absurd, deadpan idea per image. A creator alone; three cameras watching like a crew.

◈

Hardware is the star

Shoot the Osmo and phones like Sony shot the Walkman — reverent macro cut-outs, floating product, visible buttons.

07 — Deck System

Your AI Film Crew.

Two backgrounds only: Midnight opens and closes the deck; Ivory carries the content. Mono footer on every slide.

hello@dolly.crew
08 — Brand Graphics

Built from the system's own parts.

Everything here is built from the system's own pieces — feeds, brackets, buttons, timecode, pixels. No decoration that couldn't exist on Dolly's screen.

The Switch

Signature Diagram
CAM A
Feed 1
→
DOLLY
→
OUT

Pixel Mark

Social & Loading States

hi, i'm your crew

Sticker Sheet

Laptops, Cases, Booth
REC ✦ 0.0 CREW NO CREW REQUIRED 3 CAMS · 1 BRAIN 0 CREW d.
09 — Meet Dolly

The crew, embodied.

Dolly's chest carries the brand's own four buttons — so the mascot and the glyph system are one language.

d
d.

The crew, embodied.

Dolly's chest carries the brand's own four buttons — frame, record, play, and the multi-cam grid — so the mascot and the glyph system are one language. The d. monogram sits on its hip exactly where it sits in our lockups.

Where Dolly lives: social posts, onboarding, empty states, loading screens, stickers and merch — anywhere the brand talks to creators. Where it steps back: investor decks get one cameo at most; the glyphs and wordmark carry the formal moments.

Body: Midnight Indigo
Buttons & Paws: Glow Periwinkle
Face: Ink
10 — Poster Series

Your AI Film Crew.

The mascot campaign: Dolly on set, at the desk, in the edit. One idea per poster, mascot always doing a crew job.

REC 00:00:01:12 · 3 FEEDS LIVE
Looks weird.
Cuts great.
